sections.paragraph:
i.q. דָּכָא to be broken to pieces, to be crushed, once found in Kal, Psa 10:10 כתיב, וְדָכָה יָשֹׁחַ “and crushed he crouches down.” קרי, יִדְכֶּה id.

Piel, to break to pieces, to crush. Psal. 44:20 51:10 , תָּגֵלְנָה עֲצָמֹות דִּכִּיתָ “that the bones (which) thou hast broken may rejoice,” i.e. broken by a consciousness of guilt.

Niphal, pass. to be broken, crushed, Psal. 38:9 used of the heart, Psal. 51:19 , לֵב נִשְׁבָּר וְנִדְכֶּה “a broken and a contrite heart.” Hence דֳּכִי.
